# GitHub Copilot Workspace Instructions

<!-- 
  This file is automatically loaded as context for GitHub Copilot in this workspace.
  Generated by: SpecDD Starter Kit Setup Wizard (2026-03-31)

## Project Identity

**Project:** Todo Azure App Service  
**Purpose:** A task management app for managing to-do items hosted on Azure App Service  
**Primary Users:** End User

> **Default model:** gpt-4o

---

## SpecDD Workflow Commands

**Governance setup** (run once per governance level, highest first):

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/sdd-blueprint` | Generate BU-level reference architectures and standards (Level 2) |
| `/sdd-domain-spec` | Generate domain namespace spec and shared models (Level 3) |
| `/sdd-constitution` | Generate product governing principles (Level 4) |
| `/sdd-context-map` | Map bounded contexts and their relationships |

**Feature workflow** (run for each feature):

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/sdd-specify` | Create a functional specification for a feature |
| `/sdd-plan` | Generate a technical implementation plan |
| `/sdd-tasks` | Break down spec + plan into actionable tasks |
| `/sdd-implement` | Execute implementation tasks one by one |

**Optional enhancement commands:**

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/sdd-clarify` | Resolve ambiguities in a spec before planning |
| `/sdd-checklist` | Validate spec/plan quality |
| `/sdd-analyze` | Cross-check spec, plan, and tasks before implementing |
| `/sdd-code-review` | Comprehensive code review against spec, governance, and quality |
| `/sdd-adr` | Record an Architectural Decision |
| `/sdd-spike` | Run a time-boxed technical investigation |

---

## Agent Behavior

Behavior rules are defined in `.github/instructions/agent-behavior.instructions.md` and load automatically.

Governance loading order and Context references are in `.github/instructions/governance.instructions.md`.





## Tooling & DevOps Context

**Source control:** GitHub
**Project tracking / CI:**
- GitHub Actions
- Azure DevOps (ADO)
**Azure DevOps:** Before every pipeline, CI/CD, or work-item task, read `.github/instructions/azure-devops-pipelines.instructions.md`. Branch names follow `feature/[id]-short-desc`. Pipelines are YAML (`azure-pipelines.yml` or `.azure-pipelines/`). Work item hierarchy: Epics → Features → Stories → Tasks.
**GitHub Actions:** Before every CI/CD workflow task, read `.github/instructions/github-actions.instructions.md`. Workflow files go in `.github/workflows/`. Use OIDC for cloud auth, pin action versions to SHA or major tag, and set `permissions: contents: read` by default.
